code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test28-OCT-2015Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine, low calcium, and high chloride, suggesting possible renal impairment or dehydration. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, raising concern for renal dysfunction and possible metabolic derangement. Low HbA1c and absence of infection markers (nitrite, leukocytes) are noted. Recommend further evaluation for renal function, metabolic status, and possible underlying causes.
